database_adapter.decision: Define DatabaseAdapter as an abstract Effect Service that both the existing sync SQLite backend and async PgClient implement
database_adapter.interface: DatabaseAdapter { query<T>(fn: (db: DrizzleLikeClient) => T): Effect<T, AdapterError>; transaction<T>(fn: (db: DrizzleLikeClient) => T, opts?: { isolation?: string }): Effect<T, AdapterError> }

existing_patterns_to_wrap.session_session_ts_db_helper: Effect.sync(() => Database.use(fn)) at session/session.ts:507-508 — wraps in Effect.sync; PgClient would return Effect natively, so DatabaseAdapter returns Effect in both cases
existing_patterns_to_wrap.account_repo_ts_query: Effect.try({ try: () => Database.use(f) }) at account/repo.ts:49-53 — uses Effect.try for sync exceptions; DatabaseAdapter returns typed Effect failures (AdapterError), allowing caller to use Effect.catchAll uniformly

rationale: Bridges the sync/async call-pattern mismatch identified by claim-adversary (Finding H)
All 36+ call sites convert in ONE coordinated refactor with a single abstraction boundary
After conversion, the usePostgres feature flag is a trivial routing decision at the adapter level, not duplicated if/else at every call site
The adapter wraps the existing synchronous SQLite path in Effect.sync (no behavioral change) while the Postgres path returns Effect natively

desktop_postgres.decision: Use PGlite (@electric-sql/pglite) as the embedded Postgres for desktop/local deployment
rationale: PGlite embeds Postgres via WASM — no Docker, no system Postgres install required
Bundles with the app: offline-capable, zero-infrastructure desktop UX
Drizzle pg-core works with PGlite via standard postgres wire protocol (pglite exposes a pg client interface)
Avoids the Docker Compose dependency for local development — pglite runs in-process
Falls back gracefully: if PGlite is unavailable, usePostgres=false keeps SQLite path
PGlite DDL compatibility must be verified in Phase 0 (run Drizzle pg-core schema generation against PGlite and assert all tables create)
desktop_postgres.ci_strategy: GitHub Actions services.postgres:16 for CI tests. Local dev uses PGlite.

sync_event_concurrency.decision: Use REPEATABLE READ isolation with SELECT ... FOR UPDATE on EventSequenceTable to serialize aggregate access
sync_event_concurrency.sqlite_equivalent: SQLite behavior: 'immediate' transaction acquires write lock at BEGIN, serializing all access (sync/index.ts:151-169)

postgres_strategy.isolation_level: REPEATABLE READ (not SERIALIZABLE — too expensive for event writes)
postgres_strategy.locking: SELECT ... FOR UPDATE on EventSequenceTable row for the aggregate_id being written
postgres_strategy.retry: Up to 3 retries with exponential backoff on serialization_failure (40001)
postgres_strategy.connection_pool: Single writer connection for event sequencer (avoids deadlock); reader connections can pool freely

sync_event_concurrency.comment: The read+increment+write of the sequence counter is the critical section. SELECT FOR UPDATE on the aggregate_id row serializes access to that aggregate. Under REPEATABLE READ, a concurrent transaction reading the same row will block (not see a snapshot) because FOR UPDATE takes a row lock. If a deadlock occurs, Postgres kills one transaction; the retry loop handles it.
sync_event_concurrency.confirms: Postgres row-level locking makes this safe at high concurrency without the global write-lock cost of SQLite's 'immediate' transactions

column_type_mappings.$onUpdate(() => Date.now()): App-layer trigger (no Postgres DDL equivalent); session.ts sets time_updated on mutation
column_type_mappings.text({ mode: 'json' }): jsonb() in pg-core (11 columns across session, project, workspace, event schemas)

phases.name: Evidence Inventory
outputs: docs/json/database_migration_caller_inventory.v1.json — enumerating all import sites, call sites, event persistence gates, effect-drizzle coupling files
docs/json/database_migration_schema_diff.v1.json — column-by-column diff of cumulative SQLite schema vs Postgres initial migration output
docs/json/database_migration_effect_drizzle_audit.v1.json — classification of each effect-drizzle-sqlite file as keep/replace/abstract
PGlite DDL compatibility check: run `drizzle-kit generate` with pg-core definitions, apply to PGlite, verify all tables
phases.next_blocker: Phase 1 requires DatabaseAdapter interface design (architectural_decisions.database_adapter) and Phase 3 requires sync concurrency design (architectural_decisions.sync_event_concurrency) — both are resolved above

phases.name: DatabaseAdapter + Service Conversion
description: 1a. Define DatabaseAdapter Effect Service interface (architectural_decisions.database_adapter)
1b. Implement SQLiteAdapter wrapping existing Database.use/transaction in Effect.sync
1c. Implement PgClientAdapter wrapping drizzle-orm/pg-core in Effect (async)
1d. Add usePostgres feature flag routing at the adapter level
1e. Convert ALL 36+ call sites in ONE coordinated refactor commit
1f. Add Postgres service container to CI workflow for adapter tests
1g. Create test/fixture/pg.ts for Postgres-backed Effect service tests
phases.note: One coordinated refactor commit, not per-service incremental. The adapter boundary means the refactor is mechanical (wrap in DatabaseAdapter.query() instead of Database.use()), not architectural.

phases.name: Postgres Schema + Data Migration
phases.admission_condition: Phase 1 complete — DatabaseAdapter live with usePostgres=false as default
description: 2a. Create parallel pgTable definitions alongside sqliteTable in separate files (session.pg.sql.ts, session.sqlite.sql.ts)
2b. Extract shared column definitions to common file to prevent drift
2c. Generate Postgres initial migration via drizzle-kit (dialect: postgresql)
2d. Column-by-column diff of generated migration vs cumulative SQLite schema
2e. Write SQLite→Postgres data migration script (opencode db migrate-to-pg, idempotent, ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING)
2f. Add CI lint rule that pgTable columns match sqliteTable columns

phases.name: Postgres Primary, SQLite Fallback
phases.admission_condition: Phase 2 complete + sync event concurrency design accepted
description: 3a. Enable usePostgres=true as default
3b. Implement SELECT ... FOR UPDATE on EventSequenceTable in sync/index.ts event writer
3c. Add retry loop for serialization failures (max 3, exponential backoff)
3d. Remove experimentalWorkspaces gating from event persistence
3e. Validate JSON file store (Storage.Service) coexistence
3f. Integration tests for concurrent event writes against Postgres
phases.escape_hatch: usePostgres=false at startup reverts to SQLite

phases.name: DuckDB Analytical Sidecar (Stretch)
phases.admission_condition: Postgres has served 30 calendar days without data-loss regression
description: 4a. Build batch export CLI: opencode analytics export --since
4b. Derive DuckDB schema from actual tool query patterns (read report.ts, inspect_failure.ts, checkpoint.ts)
4c. Benchmark DuckDB write concurrency before committing to concurrent ingestion
4d. No DuckDB dependency in critical path

phases.name: SQLite Removal
phases.admission_condition: All of: (a) Postgres served 30 calendar days without data-loss regression, (b) all 22 call site files migrated, (c) CI runs Postgres for all tests, (d) PGlite verified for desktop deployment
description: Delete db.bun.ts, db.node.ts, json-migration.ts, 20 migration folders
Remove drizzle-orm/bun-sqlite and drizzle-orm/node-sqlite dependencies
Remove #db import alias from package.json imports
Clean up test/fixture/db.sqlite.ts references

previous_critic_findings_addressed.claim_adversary_H_incremental_big_bang: DatabaseAdapter Effect service as single abstraction boundary — all 36+ call sites convert in one coordinated refactor
previous_critic_findings_addressed.authority_adversary_F1_pgclient_interfac: DatabaseAdapter interface explicitly defined with query/transaction signatures wrapping both sync (Effect.sync) and async (Effect) paths
previous_critic_findings_addressed.authority_adversary_F8_sync_event_concur: REPEATABLE READ + SELECT FOR UPDATE + retry loop — explicit design with SQLite equivalent mapping
previous_critic_findings_addressed.authority_adversary_F4_desktop_postgres: PGlite as explicit embedded Postgres choice, with DDL compatibility check in Phase 0
previous_critic_findings_addressed.evidence_adversary_onupdate_jsonb_boolea: column_type_mappings table with explicit: $onUpdate → app-layer trigger, text({mode:'json'}) → jsonb(), integer({mode:'boolean'}) → boolean()
previous_critic_findings_addressed.authority_adversary_CI_test_harness: CI Postgres service container + test/fixture/pg.ts in Phase 1
